notes: Intel value mobile CPU brand (Pentium M-derived); launched Jan 2004 at 1.3 GHz; successor to mobile Celeron; replaced by Celeron on Core architecture 2008-2009
Banias/Dothan/Yonah low-power x86 CPU family; basis of Centrino platform; directly ancestral to Intel Core microarchitecture (2006)
Intel marketing brand for Pentium M + integrated chipset + Wi-Fi bundle; launched Mar 2003; retired as Centrino brand 2010
AMD first desktop 64-bit CPU (Sep 2003, K8 microarchitecture); brought AMD64 ISA to consumer desktops ahead of Intel EM64T
AMD 64-bit extension of x86 (Opteron Apr 2003, Athlon 64 Sep 2003); Intel adopted as Intel 64 (EM64T) 2004; became dominant 64-bit desktop/server ISA
Portable clamshell PCs; thin-and-light category emerged 2003-2005; overtook desktops in unit sales ~2008
